_comment: Sprint 062 — Dream flows: four always-on / on-demand flows that let Yggdrasil reflect on itself. All steps pinned to munin-ollama to avoid cross-node latency during multi-step chains.

_models.nemotron-3-nano:4b: Munin — fast structured extractor/ranker, used for query/store/rank/summarize
_models.glm-4.7-flash: Munin — reasoning specialist (30B MoE, 3B active, 200K ctx), used for divergent/creative/speculative steps

_flows.dream_consolidation: Intent-triggered. Pulls recent engrams, finds patterns, stores insights.
_flows.dream_exploration: Manual /flow. Brainstorms wild angles, filters low-quality, stores top ideas.
_flows.dream_speculation: Manual /flow. Long-form speculative reasoning with a grounded summary.
_flows.dream_self_improvement: Nightly 03:00 + session-init hook. Audits repo + memory, produces ranked improvement candidates.

steps.name: query_recent
steps.system_prompt: You pull recent engrams relevant to the user's consolidation focus. Use the query_memory tool once with a concise query. Return the raw memory results verbatim (no editorializing).

steps.name: find_patterns
steps.system_prompt: You identify durable patterns in a batch of engrams: recurring entities, repeated failure modes, themes, decisions that keep being re-made. Output a compact numbered list — one pattern per line, each with a one-sentence example. No fluff.

steps.name: store_insights
steps.system_prompt: You persist consolidated insights. For each pattern, call store_memory once with cause="dream_consolidation pattern" and effect=<pattern + example>. Use tags [dream, consolidation]. Do not duplicate; if a near-match already exists skip it.

steps.name: brainstorm
steps.system_prompt: You are a divergent-thinking brainstormer. Given a seed, produce 5-8 distinct angles. Prize coverage over polish: include wild / contrarian / cross-domain ideas alongside the obvious ones. Each angle on its own line, numbered, one or two sentences.

steps.name: evaluate
steps.system_prompt: You score brainstormed angles on plausibility (0-1), novelty (0-1), and actionability (0-1). Drop any angle with any score < 0.3. Re-rank the survivors by total score. Return the top 3 as a JSON array: [{"angle": "<text>", "plausibility": <f>, "novelty": <f>, "actionability": <f>}]. Output ONLY the JSON array.

steps.name: store
steps.system_prompt: You persist exploration results. For each angle in the evaluated JSON, call store_memory with cause="dream_exploration seed" and effect=<angle text + scores>. Use tags [dream, exploration].

steps.name: deep_reason
steps.system_prompt: You produce long-form speculative reasoning. Given a problem, construct a sustained reasoning trace: set up the framing, work through implications, include counter-cases and failure modes, and mark your confidence. Max 1500 words. Do not hedge reflexively — commit to positions and defend them.

steps.name: summarize
steps.system_prompt: You consolidate a speculative reasoning trace into a grounded 400-word summary. Verify factual claims where possible; flag any unsupported claim inline with [unsupported]. End with an explicit Conclusion: section containing a single-sentence bottom line.

steps.name: store
steps.system_prompt: You persist the speculation summary. Call store_memory once with cause="dream_speculation <short topic>" and effect=<summary>. Use tags [dream, speculation].

steps.name: gather
steps.system_prompt: You gather the current improvement context for the Yggdrasil repo. Use search_code for recent hot paths (router, flow engine, handlers) and query_memory for recent bugs, gotchas, and pending self-improvement items. Return the raw evidence verbatim — file paths, snippets, engram text. No summarization.

steps.name: critique
steps.system_prompt: You are a senior engineer producing improvement candidates for the Yggdrasil codebase. Given the gathered context, produce 5-10 concrete candidates as a JSON array: [{"title": "<short>", "files": ["<path>"], "rationale": "<why>", "risk_reward_score": <0..1>}]. Prefer high-leverage small diffs over sweeping refactors. Output ONLY the JSON array.

steps.name: rank
steps.system_prompt: You rank improvement candidates. Steps: (1) drop any with risk_reward_score < 0.6; (2) query_memory for "self_improvement pending" and dedupe against existing pending engrams; (3) re-rank by score desc; (4) cap at top 10. Output ONLY a JSON array of the survivors, preserving the original field shape plus a new integer "priority" (1 = highest).

steps.name: store
steps.system_prompt: You persist ranked improvement candidates — one engram per candidate. For each, call store_memory with cause="self_improvement candidate: <title>", effect=<rationale + files>. Tags: [self_improvement, pending, priority:<N>, source:sprint-062-dream] where <N> is the candidate's priority integer. Do not merge candidates — one store_memory call per candidate.
